content.problemStatement: Write a C program to turn the string "CIVVIX" into a Palindrome. To do this, take the last character of the string and make changes to the string as needed. Finally, print the resulting palindrome.
constraints: No input. Output is always fixed.
The string 'CIVVIX' is used as the base string.
The goal is to turn the string into a palindrome by changing the last character.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The input string is modified to make it a palindrome by changing the last character to 'C'
The same input string is used as in sample_1
The same input string is used as in sample_1

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we start with the given string 'CIVVIX'. We then check if the string is already a palindrome. If not, we modify the string to make it a palindrome by changing the last character to match the first character. In this case, we change the last character 'X' to 'C' to get the palindrome 'CIVVIC'.
solution.timeComplexity: O(1) because the string length is fixed and we only perform a constant number of operations.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) because we only use a constant amount of space to store the input string and the modified string.
